name: Lāna'i Lookout
tagline: Dramatic volcanic coastline lookout between Hanauma Bay and Halona Blowhole — views of Molokai and Lana'i on clear days.
bestTime: Morning for the clearest views of neighbor islands. Sunrise is spectacular here
parking: Small roadside pulloff on Kalanianaole Hwy; comes up fast so watch for it
talkStory: Most people blow past this lookout on the way to Hanauma Bay or the Halona Blowhole without stopping, which is a mistake. The volcanic rock formations here are some of the most dramatic on the island — layers of tuff and ash from the Koko Crater eruptions 30,000–300,000 years ago. On clear days you can see Molokai and Lana'i across the Kaiwi Channel, which is how it got its name. It's also a popular spot for watching humpback whales during winter months (Dec–Mar). Takes 5 minutes to enjoy and costs nothing. Stack it with Halona Blowhole (0.5 miles east) and Hanauma Bay for an East Side morning.
